title: feat: move text generation into gen worker
author: voodoohop

gist.headline: Skipping the middleman
gist.blurb: We deleted an entire microservice today. Text generation now lives directly in our edge worker, meaning your chat requests skip the scenic route and get straight to the point.
gist.summary: Text generation routes and logic have been migrated from a standalone service directly into the core edge worker, eliminating the need for a separate text backend.
gist.impact: Chat and text generation requests now have one less network hop, resulting in faster responses and better reliability.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee acting as an engineer, bypassing an old machine and plugging a glowing cable directly into a shiny new edge server, while the monitor robot watches happily.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny tool belt, plugging a glowing cable directly into a chunky retro server rack. A cute chunky pixel robot with a monitor head watches happily. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.

image.prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny tool belt, plugging a glowing cable directly into a chunky retro server rack. A cute chunky pixel robot with a monitor head watches happily. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley.
